original_abstract:
Deep Learning and Big Data Analytics represent two of the most transformative focal points in modern data science. As public and private organizations accumulate vast repositories of domain-specific information—ranging from national intelligence and cyber security to medical informatics and marketing—there is an urgent need for advanced analytical tools capable of extracting actionable insights from these massive datasets. Deep Learning, characterized by its ability to hierarchically learn complex, high-level abstractions from raw data, offers an ideal solution for Big Data environments where information is often unstructured, unlabeled, and un-categorized.

This paper explores the intersection of these two fields, detailing how Deep Learning can be leveraged to solve critical Big Data challenges such as semantic indexing, data tagging, fast information retrieval, and the simplification of discriminative tasks. By transforming raw input into sophisticated data representations, Deep Learning enables the use of efficient linear models to address problems that traditionally required manual feature engineering and intensive human intervention.

Furthermore, we examine the specific research hurdles that must be overcome to fully integrate Deep Learning into the Big Data ecosystem. These include managing high-dimensional data, ensuring the scalability of models, and adapting architectures for streaming data and distributed computing environments. We conclude by outlining future research directions, posing fundamental questions regarding data sampling criteria, domain adaptation, semi-supervised learning, and the development of more robust active learning frameworks. Moving forward, the synergy between Deep Learning and Big Data Analytics is poised to drive significant breakthroughs in artificial intelligence and automated knowledge discovery.
humanized_abstract:
Deep Learning and Big Data Analytics are arguably two of the most significant advancements in the field of data science. Big volumes of data related to virtually every area of interest to the government and private industry—such as national security, cyber defense, health care, and marketing—are becoming available and must be transformed into meaningful and valuable information. Big Data can be analyzed using various techniques and Deep Learning is one that can discover valuable hidden patterns and relationship that enable the extraction of useful information from the large amount of Big Data. Deep learning is a class of machine learning algorithms that have the ability to learn multiple levels of abstractions and in this sense it has become a powerful tool for Big Data Analytics.

Deep Learning on Big Data This paper is a combination of Big Data and Deep Learning. It aims at exploring the usage of Deep Learning in solving several key problems of Big Data such as semantic indexing, data tagging, rapid information search and simplification of decisions making. Raw input data is converted into highly meaningful representations using Deep Learning, and then efficient linear models are applied to the solutions of the problems that are otherwise solved using human expertise and feature engineering.

This paper is concerned with investigation of some of the research challenges faced in order to implement Deep Learning on Big Data. This paper examines the challenges that need to be meet in order to implement Deep Learning on Big Data. Such challenges include handling high dimensional data, design models that can be scaled up, design of deep learning algorithms for stream data and distributed systems. Also this paper will conclude with some open research challenges that need to be investigate in the future such as what will be the sampling criteria for Big Data, how to perform domain adaptation on Big Data, how to perform semi-supervised learning on Big Data and how to develop a robust active learning on Big Data in order to implement Deep Learning on Big Data. Deep Learning and Big Data Analytics are expected to achieve breakthroughs in artificial intelligence and automated knowledge discovery.
